answer:
In *Traité de la propriété* Vol. II, Charles Comte sets out the distinction between literary property and monopoly in Chapter 35.[^1]

He begins by noting the common error of confusing legal **guarantees** for literary property with the **creation of monopolies**, which has led to artificial time limits on authors' rights.[^2] He defines a monopoly as "nothing other than the prohibition made, under more or less severe penalties, to all classes of the population, from engaging in a particular kind of industry or commerce, accompanied by an exception for the profit of one or several persons."[^3] By contrast, a literary guarantee (like property rights in general) applies equally to everyone and recognizes pre-existing rights, rather than inventing privileges.[^4]

Comte's explicit **criterion** for distinguishing the two is as follows:

> It is easy, moreover, to reduce to very simple terms the differences that exist between the guarantee given to a property and the creation of a monopoly. **The establishment of a guarantee supposes... a pre-existing right; the formation of a monopoly supposes the existence of no prior exclusive right. The guarantee recognizes for all the same rights; it constitutes the regime of equality before the law; it renders to each his own. The monopoly, on the contrary, strikes the mass of the population in the exercise of its rights; it creates exceptions and constitutes privileges; it establishes the regime of inequality.**[^5]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. II, Chapter 35 ("Distinction between literary property and monopoly"), [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/).

[^2]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-445aca](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-445aca).

[^3]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-fdcbf9](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-fdcbf9).

[^4]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-3bed07](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-3bed07); [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-993bd5](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-993bd5).

[^5]: [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-d3277d](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/35-distinction-entre-la-propriete-litteraire-et-le-mo/translation/#p-d3277d).
